articles.description: Iran warns workers to leave US Israellinked firms amid retaliation threat

topStories.title: How close is the US to a quagmire in Iran?
topStories.description: The US has a 15-point plan to end the war on Iran, but what does the military reality show?

topStories.title: Mexico, Cuba Searching for Missing Aid Vessels as Island Reels
topStories.description: Mexico and Cuba are trying to find two small vessels that were delivering aid to the island as part of growing solidarity missions in the face of heightened US pressure on the government in Havana.

topStories.title: Tracing Puducherry’s voting pattern in the past three electoral battles
topStories.description: The AINRC remains the mainstay of the Union Territory’s political landscape, a position once occupied by the Congress. The decision of businessman Jose Charles Martin — who has deep pockets — to venture into electoral politics may have changed the dynamics of the 2026 election in Puducherry

topStories.title: Iran’s football team honours children killed in Minab school airstrike
topStories.description: Iranian football players laid backpacks on a football pitch in Turkey to pay tribute to the children killed in a US-Isra

topStories.title: ECI suspends police officer for failing to tackle BJP-Trinamool Congress clashes
topStories.description: The poll panel described it as "serious negligence and dereliction of duty" on the part of the officer.

topStories.title: JD Vance leads first anti-fraud task force meeting on social programmes
topStories.description: Vance, speaking Friday before the task force held a closed-door meeting, said that the federal government for decades had not taken the issue of fraud seriously and that it needed to be tackled with ‘a whole-government approach.’

topStories.title: US-Based Burger Chain Awards Nearly Rs 15,00,00,000 To Employees In Bonuses. Here's Why
topStories.description: Five Guys said the bonuses are meant to recognise the "grit and dedication" of its employees

topStories.title: Iran Says Fresh US-Israeli Strikes Targeted Nuclear Sites; No Radiation Leak Reported
topStories.description: Strikes hit a heavy-water plant and a yellowcake production facility, Iranian news agencies reported.

topStories.title: Iran Football Team Hold School Bags In Protest Over Children Killed In School Bombing
topStories.description: Iran said more than 175 people, including 165 girls and their teachers, were killed on the first day of the joint US-Israeli strikes on the country.

topStories.title: ‘Humanitarian catastrophe’: While the world is focused on the war in Iran, something much worse is unfolding in Lebanon
topStories.description: Following the US-Israeli Operation Epic Fury against Iran, Israel's ground invasion of Lebanon has led to significant civilian casualties and displacement. UNICEF reports over 370,000 children have been uprooted, with many facing dire living conditions amid ongoing violence and humanitarian crises.

topStories.title: Congress releases list of 16 candidates for upcoming Puducherry elections
topStories.description: DPR Selvam will contest from Mannadipattu, while P Karthikeyan has been fielded from Oosudu.

dailyNews.title: United States only confident that about one-third of Iran’s missile arsenal, drones have been destroyed so far
dailyNews.description: Despite US-Israeli attacks aimed at crippling Iran's missile arsenal, the country continues missile launches daily. Estimates reveal only a third of missiles confirmed destroyed, while Iran asserts ongoing production and capability, posing potential risks to US interests.

dailyNews.title: Russian man, Barron Trump and ‘jealousy’: Matvei Rumiantsev assaulted woman on video call, get 4 years in jail
dailyNews.description: The case unexpectedly involved US President Donald Trump’s son Barron Trump, who told UK police he’d witnessed the attack during a video call last year.

dailyNews.title: Melania Trump calls for AI in education as US' student scores hit 20-year low: 35% 12th graders proficient in reading
dailyNews.description: Citing NAEP's 2024 national sampling, Melania Trump said that USA's average scores have reached their lowest levels in 20 years

dailyNews.title: Nixon to Trump: Pakistan’s long record as backchannel between rival powers
dailyNews.description: Islamabad reprises its role as diplomatic broker as US and Iran edge closer to wider confrontation.

dailyNews.title: Norway to add more than $11bn to defence budget over 10 years
dailyNews.description: Norway, as well as other NATO countries, has been under pressure from the US to boost defence spending.

dailyNews.title: Judge freezes Trump admin move against AI firm, fueling battle over security authority
dailyNews.description: Ruling pauses effort to sideline Anthropic while drawing backlash and support over judicial role in Pentagon policy.

dailyNews.title: Legendary captain who inspired 'The Three Musketeers' may have been found under church altar
dailyNews.description: DNA testing may reveal if a skeleton found in a Maastricht church belongs to d'Artagnan, the real French musketeer who inspired Dumas' "The Three Musketeers."

dailyNews.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Family turns down $26M from AI giant to keep farmland
dailyNews.description: The Fox News AI Newsletter gives readers the latest AI technology advancements, covering the challenges and opportunities AI presents.

dailyNews.title: Common medical condition seen years before dementia develops, study reveals
dailyNews.description: A new study links cystitis and bacterial infections to a 19% higher rate of late-onset dementia, suggesting severe infections may accelerate cognitive decline.

dailyNews.title: Russian man who assaulted woman during Barron Trump FaceTime call sentenced to 4 years
dailyNews.description: Russian MMA fighter Matvei Rumiantsev was sentenced to four years for assaulting a woman in London in an attack Barron Trump witnessed on FaceTime.

dailyNews.title: 'Ship has sailed': This is what Dems won't get in DHS deal after shunning GOP
dailyNews.description: Senate Democrats claimed a political victory as the Homeland Security shutdown nears its end, but they secured none of the reforms they desired that started the closure in the first place.

topStories.description: For 20 years, Tim O’Harrow has asked lawmakers to acknowledge that America’s food supply depends on immigrants. Politics went in the other direction.

topStories.description: A generation ago, foreign fury over the Iraq invasion often blurred into anti-Americanism. Now, some Europeans seem ready to distinguish between the president and the American people.

dailyNews.title: ‘No Kings’ Protests Across U.S. Cities: What We Know
dailyNews.description: Thousands of demonstrations against the Trump administration are scheduled to take place in cities and towns across the country on Saturday.

dailyNews.title: Can a Democrat Make Headway With Trump? New York’s Governor Is Trying.
dailyNews.description: Gov. Kathy Hochul of New York has demonstrated her ability to negotiate with the president and his team on thorny issues like immigration enforcement.

dailyNews.title: Epstein victims sue Google, Trump administration for disclosing personal information
dailyNews.description: A lawsuit filed in Northern California alleges that Google's AI features generated contact information for Epstein victims.

dailyNews.title: Brent oil tops $110 again after Chinese ships are turned away from Strait of Hormuz
dailyNews.description: Oil prices traded higher after U.S. President Donald Trump said Iran had allowed 10 oil tankers to pass through the Strait of Hormuz.

dailyNews.title: David Sacks says his time as Trump's crypto and AI czar has ended
dailyNews.description: The venture capitalist said he will still be a part of the White House's Technology committee and will help push Trump's AI plan forward.

dailyNews.title: Trump Delays Strikes on Iran's Energy Sector for 10 More Days
dailyNews.description: After nearly one month of war, the United States, Israel and Iran are continuing to trade air and drone strikes. Now President Donald Trump says they’re also trading potential peace plans but Iran is continuing to publicly dispute that any talks are underway. It comes as the president is extending a deadline he gave Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz of face attacks against its power plants. NBC’s Garrett Haake reports for TODAY from the White House.

dailyNews.title: Ambitious, authoritarian and talking to Trump? The hard-liners rising in Iran
dailyNews.description: The Israeli military has systematically killed Iran’s top leaders, so when President Donald Trump said this week that talks are being held with Iran, the biggest question was: Who exactly is the U.S. talking to

dailyNews.title: Concerns about insider trading on the Iran war are growing. Can anyone police the bets?
dailyNews.description: Fears are growing about insider trading on U.S. policy and military plans on prediction markets. But the Trump administration has been slow to crack down.

dailyNews.title: Trump Says Colombia Will Accept Deportees, Ending Tariff Standoff
dailyNews.description: The president has made immigration an early priority to start second term, with ICE making more than 900 arrests Sunday.

dailyNews.title: Trump Says He Wants to 'Clean Out' Gaza, Send Refugees to Egypt and Jordan
dailyNews.description: The president, calling the heavily bombed enclave “a demolition site,” proposed relocating Palestinians temporarily or long-term, a move rejected by Arab countries since the war began.

dailyNews.title: Beijing Signals Readiness to Talk to Trump's Team, Even Old Foes
dailyNews.description: The Chinese foreign minister spoke by phone with Marco Rubio, the new secretary of state who has been a critic of Beijing.

dailyNews.title: Canada Is Becoming a Fentanyl Exporter, and a Target for Trump
dailyNews.description: The drug is increasingly manufactured in and trafficked from the U.S.’s northern neighbor, though the numbers remain small compared with Mexico.
